vitals.location: Bento Gonçalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil

course_description.overview: The course immerses riders in Serra Gaúcha's wine country, passing vineyards, historic landmarks like Igreja Pipa and Caminhos de Pedra, with rolling hills and a challenging finish via Barracão climb and 800m final ascent.

key_segments.name: Caminhos de Pedra
key_segments.description: Traditional route with stone buildings and Italian settler landscapes.

key_segments.name: Barracão Climb
key_segments.description: Uphill stretch toward Alameda Fenavinho leading to final 800m climb.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging with rolling terrain, significant elevation through vineyard hills, and a demanding uphill finish in Brazil's scenic wine region.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Porto Alegre (PGM) or Caxias do Sul airports; arrive by Thursday or Friday before race; Saturday flights must land by 1pm for expo.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels in Bento Gonçalves; ExpoBento hosts race week activities.
logistics.registration_details: Register via gfny.com; expo open Friday 2-8pm, Saturday 10am-8pm; packet pickup mandatory.
logistics.bike_logistics: Transport bike to expo; group rides Friday/Saturday mornings.
logistics.local_considerations: Wine region with Italian culture; enjoy local gastronomy and vineyards; warm hospitality.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic road ride through Brazil's stunning Serra Gaúcha vineyards with climbs and culture.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Gran fondo enthusiasts seeking scenic challenges, wine lovers, and GFNY finishers.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique vineyard immersion, solid organization, and finisher jersey.
pro_tips: Train for rolling climbs and heat; arrive early for packet pickup.
Join group rides for course recon.
